factors.summary: The company is deleveraging through lower net borrowings and has sufficient liquidity.

evidence.text: The repatriation of non-U.S. cash balances from certain subsidiaries could have adverse tax consequences as we may be required to pay and record tax expense on those funds that are repatriated.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable with a net increase in cash and cash equivalents.

evidence.text: Net increase in cash and cash equivalents was $13.1 million in the six months ended June 30, 2019.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are decreasing, indicating cautious investment.

evidence.text: The decrease in cash used in investing activities was due to lower capital spending.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements have a minor impact as changes are offset in the financial statements.

evidence.text: Foreign Exchange We have operations in various foreign countries... changes in the value of the related currencies affect our financial statements when translated into U.S. dollars.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is considered non-material and is being contested.

evidence.text: The Company believes that there are meritorious defenses to these actions and that these actions will not have a material adverse effect upon our results of operations, cash flows or financial condition, and where appropriate, these actions are being vigorously contested.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance costs are manageable and not material.

evidence.text: Compliance with federal, state and local laws regulating the discharge of materials into the environment, or otherwise relating to the protection of the environment, did not have a material effect on capital expenditures, earnings or the competitive position of Fortune Brands during the six and three months ended June 30, 2019 and 2018.

factors.summary: Acquisition activities are ongoing, with integration in process.

evidence.text: The Company is in the process of reviewing the internal control structure of acquired businesses and, if necessary, will make appropriate changes as we incorporate our controls and procedures into those recently acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection:
(b)

Changes in Internal Control Over Financial Reporting.
